cataloging_subject.label: Early English Poems and Lives of Saints
cataloging_subject.scope_note: Frederick J. Furnivall's editorially selected and ordered anthology of thirty-six numbered Middle English verse items, represented by the repository XML as the medieval-text portion of Transactions of the Philological Society 1858, Part II. The selection draws directly on five principal physical witnesses and uses a sixth witness only to collate item 25. It is not one medieval authored work, one saint's life, one manuscript, the whole South English Legendary, or the complete 1862 printed volume. Medieval components, editorial compilation, issue and standalone title-page states, digital encoding, repository artifact, witnesses, and facsimiles remain distinct layers.

catalog_summary.title: Early English Poems and Lives of Saints
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous Middle English poets and compilers
catalog_summary.date: Medieval texts attested from the thirteenth to mid-fifteenth century; editorial compilation published 1862
catalog_summary.region: England and south-east Ireland; witness dialect regions include the south-west Midlands, Somerset, north Worcestershire, and Waterford

titles.value: Early English Poems and Lives of Saints
Early English Poems and Lives of Saints (with Those of the Wicked Birds Pilate and Judas)
The Early South-English Legendary or Lives of Saints
notes: This is the half-title encoded before the issue title leaf; it does not describe only items 9–24.
The XML preserves this issue title leaf; direct scan and DIMEV evidence place Part II's Berlin publication in 1862.

agents.name: Anonymous Middle English poets and compilers
notes: A cataloging collective for numerous distinct anonymous creators; it is not a claim of shared authorship.

agents.name: Frederick James Furnivall
notes: Editor, selector, transcriber, and compiler of the 1862 anthology.

scope.description: The thirty-six medieval components, without implying a single author

notes: No component is assigned to Michael of Kildare merely because it survives in Harley MS 913.

scope.description: Selection and ordering of thirty-six items from five principal witnesses
Direct copying of the selected manuscript texts
Egerton 613's later Conduct of Life copy for item 8 and the Vernon witness for item 25
Two Conduct of Life copies in Egerton MS 613
Selected South English Legendary texts in Harley MS 2277
Selected Kildare Lyrics in Harley MS 913
Furnivall's selection, transcription, arrangement, and apparatus
Probable origin of the relevant Egerton 613 sections
Probable production context of Harley MS 913

form_statements.description: A verse anthology; item 6 embeds two short Latin prose authorities, and modern prose editorial apparatus is not treated as the form of the medieval selection.

summaries.value: Furnivall's anthology ranges from sermons, moral lyrics, eschatological and Passion fragments, and the early Conduct of Life to sixteen selected South English Legendary narratives, six late-fourteenth-century religious songs, an imperfect anti-nunnery satire, and five Kildare Lyrics including Old Age, Earth, a satire on the people of Kildare, The Land of Cokaygne, and Five Evil Things. Its chief concerns are Christian conduct, death and judgment, Passion devotion, saints and miracles, mercy, transience, and satirical criticism of social and religious life. The collection is valuable both for the varied medieval verse it assembles and for the visible nineteenth-century practices of manuscript selection, expansion, punctuation, supply, and limited collation.

The encoded body has thirty-six numbered items on printed pages 1–161: seven Harley MS 913 items; Conduct of Life from two copies in Egerton MS 613; sixteen South English Legendary selections from Harley MS 2277; six songs from Add MS 22283, with Vernon collation only for item 25; Why I Can't Be a Nun from Cotton MS Vespasian D IX; and five further Harley MS 913 items. The XML omits the 1862 standalone title page, preface, contents, corrigenda, notes on pp. 162–165, index on pp. 166–171, and list of words on pp. 172–180 preserved in the complete scan.

The cataloged unit is Furnivall's edited selection, not a hypothetical medieval anthology. Numbered editorial parts do not erase separately indexed works, excerpts, fragments, embedded Latin, discontiguous manuscript leaves, variant witnesses, or imperfect boundaries. Witness dates and dialect regions are first-attestation evidence rather than automatically composition dates. XML L and LG counts are reproducible encoding metrics, not uniformly prosodic line and stanza counts.

extent.description: The body contains 36 numbered DIV2 items, 348 LG elements, and 5,615 L elements. These are encoding metrics: many long poems occupy one LG; item 32 compresses about 78 short lines into 19 typographic L rows; item 33 compresses Latin metrical text; item 35 includes an editorial loss notice as one L; and items 5, 13, 17, 19, 24, and 31 have resets, gaps, starred numbers, or offsets. The XML also contains 375 NOTE elements and 163 body PB milestones; item 36 shares printed p. 161 and has no separate PB.

content_parts.label: 1. A Sarmun
content_parts.summary: A moral sermon against pride and covetousness, on death, hell, and heaven.
notes: Sixty four-line groups; the edition splits number 2 into 2a and 2b.

content_parts.label: 2. XV Signa ante Iudicium (fragment)
content_parts.summary: A fragmentary Middle English account of the Fifteen Signs before Judgment, ending during the twelfth day.
notes: The XML uses one LG for 180 lines; printed pp. 162–164 add a comparison text that is outside the encoded work boundary.

content_parts.label: 3. The Fall and Passion
content_parts.summary: A poem on the Fall and Christ's Passion.
notes: The XML uses one LG for 108 lines.

content_parts.label: 4. The Ten Commandments
content_parts.summary: A moral poem on the commandments, including swearing and Sunday trading.
notes: The XML has forty long L rows; DIMEV describes twenty quatrains and notes two verse lines written per manuscript line.

content_parts.label: 5. Fragment on the Seven Sins
content_parts.summary: A discontinuous and incomplete moral poem on the seven deadly sins.
notes: Ten numbered six-line stanzas are followed by a large unnumbered group whose line numbering restarts; the text ends abruptly.

content_parts.label: 6. Christ on the Cross (fragment)
content_parts.summary: A composite numbered item joining two devotional poems on the crucified Christ with Latin scriptural framing.
notes: Furnivall's one numbered item combines two separately indexed Middle English verse texts and two Latin prose authorities; it is not modeled as one medieval composition.

content_parts.label: 7. A Rhyme-Beginning Fragment
content_parts.summary: A twelve-line fragment whose successive lines begin with the preceding line's rhyme.
notes: Three quatrains; DIMEV treats the passage as possibly the conclusion of a longer work.

content_parts.label: 8. A Moral Ode
content_parts.summary: The early Middle English Conduct of Life or Poema Morale, printed from one Egerton 613 copy with variants from the later copy in the same codex.

locators.value: Egerton MS 613, ff. 7r–12v, collated with ff. 64r–70v

notes: The 199 XML LG elements are couplets; 258 NOTE elements carry the extensive same-codex variant apparatus.

content_parts.label: 9. St Dunstan
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary life of St Dunstan.
notes: The edition's tailnote names intervening legends but does not encode them.

content_parts.label: 10. An Oxford Student
content_parts.summary: A South English Legendary miracle of the Virgin concerning an Oxford scholar.
notes: A complete 74-line selected miracle.

content_parts.label: 11. The Jews and the Cross
content_parts.summary: A South English Legendary miracle involving Jews and the Cross.
notes: Twelve couplets in one XML LG.

content_parts.label: 12. St Swithin
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary life of St Swithin.
notes: Eighty couplets in one XML LG.

content_parts.label: 13. St Kenelm
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary life of St Kenelm.
notes: The XML has 367 L rows while the printed milestone sequence reaches 368; the discrepancy is preserved as an encoding metric, not silently repaired.

content_parts.label: 14. A Miracle of St James
content_parts.summary: A selected miracle from the South English Legendary life of St James.

locators.value: Harley MS 2277, selected passage at ff. 97r–101r

notes: This is an excerpt from the manuscript life, not the whole legend.

content_parts.label: 15. St Christopher
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary life of St Christopher.
notes: A 228-line selection in one XML LG.

content_parts.label: 16. The 11,000 Virgins
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary account of the Eleven Thousand Virgins.
notes: A 180-line selection in one XML LG.

content_parts.label: 17. St Edmund the Confessor
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary life of St Edmund of Abingdon, archbishop and confessor.
notes: The XML has 600 L rows and an inserted starred milestone 375*; later printed line numbers are therefore not a simple count of XML rows.

content_parts.label: 18. St Edmund the King
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary life of St Edmund, king and martyr.
notes: Fifty-one couplets in one XML LG.

content_parts.label: 19. St Katherine
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary life of St Katherine.
notes: The XML has 309 L rows but the printed sequence ends at 305 after editorially irregular numbering.

content_parts.label: 20. St Andrew
content_parts.summary: An imperfect South English Legendary life of St Andrew following St Katherine.
notes: The selected text ends imperfectly.

content_parts.label: 21. Seinte Lucie
content_parts.summary: A selected South English Legendary life of St Lucy.
notes: Eighty-seven couplets in one XML LG.

content_parts.label: 22. St Edward: A Miracle of St John
content_parts.summary: A twenty-two-line selected miracle concerning St Edward and St John.

locators.value: Harley MS 2277, selected passage beginning around f. 195

notes: The edition extracts one miracle rather than transcribing the whole source life.

content_parts.label: 23. Judas Iscariot
content_parts.summary: A South English Legendary account of Judas Iscariot.
notes: Seventy-three couplets in one XML LG.

content_parts.label: 24. Pilate
content_parts.summary: A South English Legendary account of Pilate.
notes: The XML has 261 L rows and a starred milestone 60*; DIMEV's reported end at f. 234 conflicts with the cataloged codex extent of f. 232v.

content_parts.label: 25. A Song of Mercy
content_parts.summary: A sixteen-stanza moral song whose refrain says that mercy passes all things.

locators.value: Add MS 22283, f. 128vb–129ra; collated with Vernon MS f. 407ra–c

notes: Furnivall supplies four lines from Vernon and records eighty variant notes; this is the only one of items 25–30 that he collated against Vernon.

content_parts.label: 26. A Song of Deo Gracias
content_parts.summary: An eleven-stanza moral song with a Latin Deo gracias refrain.
notes: Eleven eight-line stanzas; Furnivall explicitly says he did not compare this or the following songs with Vernon.

content_parts.label: 27. Deus Caritas
content_parts.summary: A seven-stanza religious song with a Latin opening and refrain.
notes: Seven eight-line stanzas.

content_parts.label: 28. Another Song of Deo Gracias
content_parts.summary: A six-stanza moral and devotional song with Latin refrain.
notes: Six eight-line stanzas.

content_parts.label: 29. A Song: Know Thyself
content_parts.summary: A nine-stanza moral song urging self-knowledge.
notes: Nine twelve-line stanzas.

content_parts.label: 30. A Song of Yesterday
content_parts.summary: A fifteen-stanza moral meditation on transience.
notes: Fifteen twelve-line stanzas.

content_parts.label: 31. Why I Can't Be a Nun
content_parts.summary: An imperfect mid-fifteenth-century satirical complaint about female religious life.

locators.value: Cotton MS Vespasian D IX, ff. 177–182 and misbound f. 190

notes: The witness begins and ends imperfectly; the dislocated damaged leaf is part of the same medieval text.

content_parts.label: 32. Old Age
content_parts.summary: A first-person complaint on bodily decline in old age.
notes: The XML's nineteen long typographic L rows contain about seventy-eight short verse lines; L count is not a prosodic line count.

content_parts.label: 33. Earth
content_parts.summary: A bilingual meditation on death in alternating Middle English and Latin stanzas.
notes: Seven English six-line stanzas alternate with seven Latin rendering stanzas; the XML's Latin rows compress multiple metrical units.

content_parts.label: 34. Of Men Lif That Woneth in Land
content_parts.summary: A twenty-stanza social satire on the people of Kildare.
notes: Twenty six-line stanzas; no named authorship is assigned from the manuscript's broader Kildare context.

content_parts.label: 35. The Land of Cokaygne
content_parts.summary: A satirical Middle English vision of an earthly paradise and corrupt religious life.
notes: The XML has 191 L elements because the bracketed editorial statement '[Here a few lines seem to be lost.]' occupies one L; DIMEV describes ninety-five couplets.

content_parts.label: 36. Five Evil Things
content_parts.summary: A seven-line gnomic list of five forms of bad rule or conduct.
notes: This item shares printed p. 161 with the end of item 35 and therefore has no separate PB milestone in the XML.

related_works.title: South English Legendary
notes: Items 9–24 are selected legends or passages, not a complete edition of the legendary.

evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD, IDG, FRONT, BODY/DIV1 and all thirty-six DIV2 elements
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for repository identity, encoded issue half-title and title leaf, thirty-six-item order, manuscript citations, textual content, printed-page milestones, notes, supplied matter, and 2016 review history.

evidence.citation: Structural, pagination, note-distribution, numbering, and scan-boundary audit of CME00116.xml.
evidence.locator: DIV1/DIV2, HEAD, LG, L, P, NOTE, PB, MILESTONE, first and final lines of every numbered item
evidence.summary: Confirms 413,452 bytes; three DIV1, thirty-six DIV2, 353 HEAD, 348 LG, 5,615 L, 375 NOTE, 62 P, and 163 body PB elements; exact item page spans; note concentration in items 8 and 25; all line-number resets and starred offsets; and the absent PB for item 36. ScanData and OCR comparison identifies complete edition pp. 1–161 and duplicate scan captures of pp. 54–55.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Early English Poems and Lives of Saints (Berlin: A. Asher & Co., 1862).
evidence.summary: Direct title-page, preface, contents, text, apparatus, and boundary evidence for Furnivall's editor/compiler role, five principal witnesses, Vernon collation only for item 25, editorial expansions and punctuation, and complete printed-volume structure.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scan structure for EarlyEnglishPoemsLivesOfSaints.
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete 226-image 1862 scan, exact derivative inventory, public-domain mark, and page-level boundary data.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records associated with all thirty-six numbered items.
evidence.summary: Versioned scholarly evidence for DIMEV/IMEV identifiers, titles and incipits, verse forms, witness loci, selected or imperfect status, and Furnivall bibliography.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 913.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the Kildare Lyrics codex's date, probable Waterford origin, languages, extent, hands, physical form, and complete public IIIF viewer.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 613 and its two Conduct of Life components.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the composite codex, both Conduct of Life copies, their dates and loci, scribal sections, and complete public IIIF viewer.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2277.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the c. 1300 South English Legendary witness, its physical extent, origin, script, and current access limits.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 22283.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the late-fourteenth-century Simeon Manuscript, its north Worcestershire association, shared Vernon scribe, and current access limits.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Vespasian D IX, ff. 177–182 and f. 190.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the mid-fifteenth-century imperfect anti-nunnery satire, its separated f. 190 continuation, language, date, and request route.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries catalog and ORA complete facsimile for MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, the Vernon Manuscript.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the Vernon witness at f. 407ra–c, manuscript date and origin, scribal relationship to Add MS 22283, complete 4.3 GB public facsimile, and provider reuse terms.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00116.
evidence.summary: Official title, contributor, publication, stable identifier, public access, and No Copyright–United States statement for the digital item.

open_questions.question: What exact physical issue or bound Transactions exemplar supplied the XML's half-title and 'Transactions ... 1858' title leaf, and how does it relate bibliographically to the complete standalone 1862 title-page state?
next_steps: Locate the U-M source-copy catalog record or imaging provenance.
Compare a complete Transactions of the Philological Society 1858 Part II issue against the standalone 1862 scan leaf by leaf.
Retain the volume year 1858 and publication year 1862 separately until the issue history is documented.

open_questions.question: Which individual creators, composition dates, and original dialect regions can be assigned to the thirty-six medieval components without transferring witness-level evidence to abstract works?
next_steps: Research each DIMEV record and textual tradition independently.
Distinguish composition, surviving witness, scribal copying, Furnivall transcription, and later catalog attribution.
Do not assign Michael of Kildare to item 34 or the whole Harley 913 group without item-specific evidence.

open_questions.question: What is the exact end locus of item 24, Pilate, in Harley MS 2277?
next_steps: Inspect complete manuscript images or obtain a repository foliation statement for the final leaves.
Reconcile DIMEV's end at f. 234 with the current BL record's codex extent through f. 232v and any old foliation.

notes: Only item 25 is collated against the Vernon Manuscript; Furnivall explicitly says he had no time to compare item 26 and the following songs with the Bodleian copy.
The complete Internet Archive scan contains a duplicated capture sequence for printed pp. 54–55; this is a reproduction-layer defect and does not create duplicate XML PB milestones or medieval text.
The XML's 375 notes are highly uneven: item 8 has 258 same-codex variant notes and item 25 has 80 Vernon collation notes.
The review entry dated 2016-08-25 records a later validation and structural proofing pass, not the date of medieval composition, nineteenth-century editing, or initial electronic keying.
